notes: Single canonical policy file for legal mode and termsheet generation.
Deterministic rules live here; retrieval context comes from shared search plane.
canonical_inputs: Internal term sheet archive (53 executed term sheets)
PortCo corporate doc archives (3 collections, 5700+ PDFs)
Paradigm deal agents operational handoff document

source_precedence.rule: Red lines, compliance checks, and clause defaults are always controlling.
Canonical internal operating context is authoritative over precedent outliers and general market summaries.
Use as evidence and drafting style guidance, but do not override higher-priority policy/context.
Use as supplemental context when not in conflict with higher-priority sources.

first_class_inputs: Internal term sheet archive (53 executed term sheets).
Preferred Stock Financing Checklist and Venture Financing Overview materials.
Internal deal-agents handoff text and current term sheet template.
usage_requirements: For drafting and review, retrieve from first-class inputs before loading broad market context.
When source conflict appears, cite both and apply source_precedence deterministically.
Never treat a single precedent outlier as policy.

red_lines.text: Anti-dilution must be BBWA; never full ratchet.
Paradigm blocking rights are required when leading/meaningful owner.
IP/token transfer treated as deemed liquidation event.
SPA includes sanctions and outbound investment reps.
MRL names Paradigm Fund LP and Paradigm Two LP.
Major Investor qualification is explicit or any variance is surfaced.
Paradigm consent required for key amendments.
Flag rights parity gaps vs other investors.
Token warrant defaults to net exercise.
Lockup terms cannot be more onerous than insiders.
Smart contract restrictions require Paradigm consent.

notes: Maintain founder relationship while preserving key protections.
Lead investor usually funds first; followers may wait for lead wire confirmation.

review_order.focus: liquidation preference, anti-dilution, protective provisions, deemed liquidation
economics, reps and warranties, closing conditions, sanctions and OISP
major investor qualification, pro rata, section 220, amendment veto
net exercise, lockup MFN, smart-contract restrictions
entity naming, rights parity, enforceability, closing completeness

conflict_resolution.rule: When higher-priority sources conflict with lower-priority sources, follow the higher-priority source and explicitly note the conflict.

retrieval_query_templates: internal canonical financing context {document_type} {clause_focus} process checklist
internal term sheet archive {stage} {intent} {document_type} drafting language

items: Cap table reconciliation on fully diluted basis.
Outstanding SAFE/convertible stack and conversion math.
Option pool sufficiency and prior-round unusual terms.
Blue sky filing route and state notice obligations.
CFIUS analysis for foreign investor exposure.
Employee and contractor IP assignments complete.
Smart contract ownership and upgrade rights.
Outstanding token rights and warrant schedule.
Smart contract audits and remediation status.

exception_patterns.pattern: Participating liquidation appears in isolated deals.
exception_patterns.action: Escalate and require explicit rationale before acceptance.

exception_patterns.pattern: Board observer used when seat not available or thresholded.
exception_patterns.action: Track parity and trigger conditions explicitly.

exception_patterns.pattern: Major Investor thresholds vary across docs.
Token warrant not present in every deal.
exception_patterns.action: Gate token checks on actual token relevance and doc set.

recurring_clause_language.default_language: Preferred majority consent plus Paradigm-sensitive controls
recurring_clause_language.negotiation_posture: Escalate missing blocking rights when lead.

recurring_clause_language.default_language: Major Investor based information and pro rata rights
Net exercise, lockup MFN, consent around restrictive changes
recurring_clause_language.negotiation_posture: Treat missing net exercise and lockup MFN as high severity.

compliance_rules.label: Paradigm blocking rights present when lead/meaningful owner
IP/token transfer treated as deemed liquidation event where applicable
Competitor carve-out excludes Paradigm affiliates
required_any: in no event shall paradigm or its affiliates be a competitor

compliance_rules.label: No waiver of DGCL Section 220 rights
Amendment veto requires Paradigm written consent
Rights parity language is preserved against other investors
MRL names Paradigm Fund LP and Paradigm Two LP
Token warrant defaults to net exercise
Lockup not more onerous than insiders
Smart contract restrictions require Paradigm consent

base.protective_provision_v_text: any interested or related party transactions other than transactions entered into in the ordinary course of business on an arm's-length basis and benefits made available to all employees

late_minority.protective_provision_v_text: any interested or related party transactions above a materiality threshold, excluding ordinary-course and broad-based employee benefit actions, with board-level disclosure to Paradigm
late_minority.other_rights_text: Customary NVCA investor rights, including information rights and registration rights. Parties should use commercially reasonable efforts to preserve rights parity where practicable for a non-lead minority position.

growth_lead.protective_provision_v_text: any interested or related party transactions other than ordinary-course arm's-length transactions and broad-based employee benefits, with Paradigm consent required for material exceptions

founder_friendly.other_rights_text: Customary NVCA investor rights with practical execution flexibility on timing and process, while preserving core Paradigm information and consent protections.
founder_friendly.vesting_text: Founder vesting subject to due diligence, with implementation timing coordinated to reduce operational disruption while preserving standard protection mechanics.

investor_protective.protective_provision_v_text: any interested or related party transactions other than ordinary-course arm's-length transactions and broad-based employee benefits, with Paradigm written consent required for exceptions

token_heavy.token_rights_text: For any Tokens (other than non-fungible or similar ordinary-course assets), Paradigm receives its pro rata share (fully diluted) of launch supply and any insider-linked inflationary issuances, subject to net exercise defaults, lockup MFN parity, and Paradigm written-consent controls for material smart-contract restriction changes.

template_docx: Template includes 1x non-participating liquidation preference and 45-day no-shop baseline.
Template includes legal fee cap convention and standard NVCA framing.
handoff_txt: Red-line framing for BBWA, blocking rights, major investor qualification, and token controls.
checklist_docx_and_gunderson_pptx: Regulatory diligence emphasis: Rule 506/Bad Actor, blue sky, HSR, CFIUS, OISP.
Definitive doc package order and closing checklist discipline.
